summary: Knowledge website redesign is in final feedback stage; Asia Start and Intelligent China programs are live and driving SEO focus; the CKGSB-IE joint report 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' is on track for a September launch in Singapore.
summaryDetail: The biggest priority right now is finalizing the knowledge website redesign: the team preferred Joker's design but wants more differentiation from the current site, with feedback due from Joseph, Julie, and team lead. Meanwhile, the Asia Start campaign launched this week with two routes (AI and traditional), and the Intelligent China program website is going live—both are the primary focus for SEO efforts, deprioritizing US partnerships. The joint report with IE on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia is progressing well: the working title is confirmed, translation is capped at 50% of the Chinese original, and op-ed contributors from both schools are being identified. Urgent this week: finalize website design feedback, launch Intelligent China webpage, and confirm event themes with Tech in Asia for the September report launch. Coming up: social media agency bidding presentations on July 15, and the Global Program Brochure needs Dean Xiang's feedback before finalization.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed two design proposals for the knowledge website, preferring Joker's version for its menu categorization but wanting more differentiation from current design. They discussed incorporating elements from Joseph's version to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Meeting
meetings.summary: Discussed SEO strategy to improve CKGSB's search ranking for programs in AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China. Prioritized Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on website menu, school brochure updates, CES quotes for social media, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency search. Concerns raised about Zhu Yang's ChatGPT-generated feedback and timeline for agency selection.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team plans a joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', to be launched in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation and Market Dynamics
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on de-risking and company operations. Emphasis on government frameworks versus company autonomy.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep school menu at top left and knowledge menu centered (preferred).
Show more articles on homepage, with pinned article at top left and latest articles below.
Bring indices (BCI, BSI) to knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO efforts.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; will be key for driving traffic.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or converting to 10-point article.
Zhu Yang's feedback is ChatGPT-generated and will be deprioritized; he is on blacklist.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of original Chinese report.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report to be ready by early September for Singapore events in mid-September.
Include industry perspective via case studies or op-ed from an alum active in Southeast Asia.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month.
Intelligent China program will target foreigners, with modules purchasable separately or together.
CKGSB X Tech In Asia partnership paperwork may not be ready within July; Xuan will follow up after vacation.
decisions.meetingTitle: 回复: 答复: CKGSB X Tech In ASIA

decisions.text: Global Marcom's report is finalized and will be sent to Shouhuo.
Clean version of Global Program Brochure to be shared with Alan first for Dean Xiang's feedback before sending to Midori.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: CKGSB - Global Program Brochure - July 2026
